findings.finding: Enterprise generative-AI spend reached an estimated $13.8B in 2024, up roughly 6x year over year, with the majority shifting from innovation/experimentation budgets into permanent line items — the demo-to-budget transition the piece argues is completing in 2026.

AI startups captured around one third of all global venture funding in 2024, and within that the application/orchestration layer — not new foundation models — is where the share of new rounds is growing fastest.

The cost to query a model at GPT-3.5 quality fell more than 280x in ~18 months (Nov 2022 to mid-2024); inference price for a fixed capability level is collapsing, pushing margin away from raw tokens and up the stack.

On long-horizon multi-step office tasks, frontier agents still complete only a minority end to end — benchmarks such as web/computer-use and multi-turn task suites put autonomous success well under half, confirming reliability (not capability) as the binding constraint.

Klarna reported its AI assistant handled the work equivalent of ~700 full-time agents and resolved customer-service chats in under 2 minutes (vs ~11 before), a named, quantified production deployment — the proof-point that agents do real work at scale.

Incumbents are repricing toward outcome/agent units: Salesforce launched Agentforce priced around $2 per resolved conversation, an explicit move from per-seat to per-outcome — the incumbent response the piece predicts.

The 'agents replace SaaS seats' thesis is real in direction but over-stated in timeline: seat counts are not collapsing yet; what is changing is pricing model and per-seat expansion. Treat full seat-replacement as still-partly-vaporware over a 2026 horizon — it qualifies the thesis rather than confirming it.

Industry analysis locates the defensible moat in the orchestration / evaluation / integration layer — context, tools, memory, guardrails and the proprietary workflow data agents run on — rather than in the model weights, which commoditize.

research_notes: Best practices applied: each figure traced to a named source; recent (2024-2025) data preferred; the replace-seats claim deliberately QUALIFIED rather than rubber-stamped so the piece stays honest for a skeptical investor. C7 is synthesized commentary, flagged as such.
source: content-research-writer step 3 — Conduct Research (Key Findings + numbered Citations; one finding KILLS/qualifies a claim to keep the piece honest)
